sanskrit:
जन्म कर्म च मे दिव्यमेवं यो वेत्ति तत्त्वतः।
त्यक्त्वा देहं पुनर्जन्म नैति मामेति सोऽर्जुन॥
transliteration:
janma karma ca me divyam evaṃ yo vetti tattvataḥ |
tyaktvā dehaṃ punar janma naiti mām eti so ’rjuna ||
literal_meaning: Birth and action of Mine—divine—thus / whoever knows in truth / having abandoned the body / again birth / does not attain / to Me / he goes / O Arjuna
contextual_meaning: When you recognize that every event in your life is a purposeful ‘incarnation’ of deeper intelligence—not random chaos—you stop recycling the same existential anxiety; the mind is freed from the compulsive need to be reborn into the same worry loops.

shadow: Doubt about being worthy of grace

psychological_state: Post-insight catharsis: the felt-sense that one’s biography is not merely personal history but sacred script; egoic time-line collapses into timeless presence.
trigger_scenario: You finally see that your panic attacks are ‘avatars’ inviting you to deeper self-knowledge; the moment you grok this, the attacks lose their grip and you feel already home.
anchor_text: See every moment as divine purpose and you stop being reborn into fear.
